methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $16M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Orchid is a Series A biotech and health company founded by Noor Siddiqui that has raised $16.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a universe of 11 comparable companies spanning both private (8 firms including Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, and Asimov) and public (3 tickers: RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) peers in the biotech and health sector.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-Progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $16M raised proxy, implying a $33M valuation consistent with typical Series A to subsequent-stage advancement. Peer-Median valuation references six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $805M, reflecting a range from $100M (low) to $3.3B (high). Public-Comp anchoring cannot be applied due to the absence of revenue data for the target company.

The two applicable methods produce a wide triangulated range: $28M (low), $419M (median), and $926M (high). The substantial spread reflects structural uncertainty inherent in early-stage biotech valuation. Stage-Progression grounds the lower bound based on capital deployed. Peer-Median dominates the upper bound and median but applies comparables with one stage advancement (Series B), introducing step-up volatility and heterogeneity in underlying clinical or commercial progress. Key unknowns are Orchid's clinical milestones, intellectual property strength, and capital efficiency relative to the peer set.
